---
title: "R Notebook"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

```{r carga de datos}
load("listings_modificado.RData")
```

```{r paquetes}
library(caret)
library(e1071)
```


```{r conjuntos de entrenamiento y prueba}
set.seed(123)

muestra_idx <- sample(1:nrow(listings), size = 10000)
muestra <- listings[muestra_idx, ]

n <- nrow(muestra)
train_idx <- sample(1:n, size = 0.7 * n)

train <- muestra[train_idx, ]
test  <- muestra[-train_idx, ]
```

```{r variable categórica}
cuts <- quantile(train$price, probs = c(0.33, 0.66), na.rm = TRUE)

train$price_cat <- cut(train$price,
                       breaks = c(-Inf, cuts[1], cuts[2], Inf),
                       labels = c("Economica", "Intermedia", "Cara"))

test$price_cat <- cut(test$price,
                      breaks = c(-Inf, cuts[1], cuts[2], Inf),
                      labels = c("Economica", "Intermedia", "Cara"))

control <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5)
```

```{r modelo 1: SVM lineal}
set.seed(123)

svm_lineal <- train(
  price_cat ~ accommodates + bathrooms_num + bedrooms + beds +
    host_response_rate + host_acceptance_rate +
    calculated_host_listings_count + number_of_reviews +
    reviews_per_month + review_scores_rating +
    minimum_nights + availability_365 +
    room_type + host_is_superhost + instant_bookable + bathroom_type,
  data = train,
  method = "svmLinear",
  trControl = control,
  tuneLength = 5,
  preProcess = c("center", "scale")
)

pred_lineal <- predict(svm_lineal, newdata = test)
cm_lineal <- confusionMatrix(pred_lineal, test$price_cat)

cm_lineal
```

```{r modelo 2: SVM radial}
set.seed(123)

svm_radial <- train(
  price_cat ~ accommodates + bathrooms_num + bedrooms + beds +
    host_response_rate + host_acceptance_rate +
    calculated_host_listings_count + number_of_reviews +
    reviews_per_month + review_scores_rating +
    minimum_nights + availability_365 +
    room_type + host_is_superhost + instant_bookable + bathroom_type,
  data = train,
  method = "svmRadial",
  trControl = control,
  tuneLength = 5,
  preProcess = c("center", "scale")
)

pred_radial <- predict(svm_radial, newdata = test)
cm_radial <- confusionMatrix(pred_radial, test$price_cat)

cm_radial
```

```{r modelo 3: SVM polinomial}
set.seed(123)

svm_poly <- train(
  price_cat ~ accommodates + bathrooms_num + bedrooms + beds +
    host_response_rate + host_acceptance_rate +
    calculated_host_listings_count + number_of_reviews +
    reviews_per_month + review_scores_rating +
    minimum_nights + availability_365 +
    room_type + host_is_superhost + instant_bookable + bathroom_type,
  data = train,
  method = "svmPoly",
  trControl = control,
  tuneLength = 3,
  preProcess = c("center", "scale")
)

pred_poly <- predict(svm_poly, newdata = test)
cm_poly <- confusionMatrix(pred_poly, test$price_cat)

cm_poly
```

```{r punto 8: comparacion entre modelos SVM}
# --- Métricas de cada modelo SVM sobre test ---
acc_lineal <- cm_lineal$overall["Accuracy"]
acc_radial <- cm_radial$overall["Accuracy"]
acc_poly   <- cm_poly$overall["Accuracy"]

kappa_lineal <- cm_lineal$overall["Kappa"]
kappa_radial <- cm_radial$overall["Kappa"]
kappa_poly   <- cm_poly$overall["Kappa"]

# Sensibilidad por clase (macro)
sens_lineal <- mean(cm_lineal$byClass[, "Sensitivity"], na.rm = TRUE)
sens_radial <- mean(cm_radial$byClass[, "Sensitivity"], na.rm = TRUE)
sens_poly   <- mean(cm_poly$byClass[,   "Sensitivity"], na.rm = TRUE)

spec_lineal <- mean(cm_lineal$byClass[, "Specificity"], na.rm = TRUE)
spec_radial <- mean(cm_radial$byClass[, "Specificity"], na.rm = TRUE)
spec_poly   <- mean(cm_poly$byClass[,   "Specificity"], na.rm = TRUE)

comparacion_svm <- data.frame(
  Modelo       = c("SVM Lineal", "SVM Radial", "SVM Polinomial"),
  Accuracy     = round(c(acc_lineal, acc_radial, acc_poly), 4),
  Kappa        = round(c(kappa_lineal, kappa_radial, kappa_poly), 4),
  Sens_Macro   = round(c(sens_lineal, sens_radial, sens_poly), 4),
  Spec_Macro   = round(c(spec_lineal, spec_radial, spec_poly), 4)
)

print(comparacion_svm)

# Errores por clase: donde se equivoca más cada modelo
cat("\n--- Errores por clase: SVM Lineal ---\n")
print(cm_lineal$table)
cat("\n--- Errores por clase: SVM Radial ---\n")
print(cm_radial$table)
cat("\n--- Errores por clase: SVM Polinomial ---\n")
print(cm_poly$table)
```

```{r punto 9: comparacion SVM vs modelos anteriores de clasificacion}
# Asume que los modelos anteriores ya existen en el environment con sus confusionMatrix:
# cm_arbol, cm_rf, cm_nb, cm_knn, cm_logistica
# Si no están cargados, cargar el .RData correspondiente antes de correr este chunk

modelos_clf <- list(
  "Arbol Decision"   = cm_arbol,
  "Random Forest"    = cm_rf,
  "Naive Bayes"      = cm_nb,
  "KNN"              = cm_knn,
  "Reg. Logistica"   = cm_logistica,
  "SVM Lineal"       = cm_lineal,
  "SVM Radial"       = cm_radial,
  "SVM Polinomial"   = cm_poly
)

resumen_clf <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(modelos_clf), function(nombre) {
  cm <- modelos_clf[[nombre]]
  data.frame(
    Modelo     = nombre,
    Accuracy   = round(cm$overall["Accuracy"], 4),
    Kappa      = round(cm$overall["Kappa"], 4),
    Sens_Macro = round(mean(cm$byClass[, "Sensitivity"], na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    Spec_Macro = round(mean(cm$byClass[, "Specificity"], na.rm = TRUE), 4)
  )
}))

rownames(resumen_clf) <- NULL
print(resumen_clf)

mejor_clf <- resumen_clf[which.max(resumen_clf$Accuracy), "Modelo"]
cat("\nMejor modelo de clasificación por Accuracy:", mejor_clf, "\n")
```

```{r punto 10: tabla de sobreajuste modelos de clasificacion}
# Para detectar sobreajuste comparamos Accuracy en TRAIN vs TEST para cada modelo.
# Un modelo sobreajustado tiene accuracy train >> accuracy test.

# Recalcular accuracy en train para los modelos SVM (caret guarda resampling)
acc_train_lineal <- max(svm_lineal$results$Accuracy)
acc_train_radial <- max(svm_radial$results$Accuracy)
acc_train_poly   <- max(svm_poly$results$Accuracy)

# Para los modelos anteriores se deben tener los objetos caret (o las predicciones en train).
# Ejemplo genérico: reemplazar con los valores reales de cada hoja anterior.
# acc_train_arbol, acc_train_rf, etc.  — completar con valores guardados

sobreajuste <- data.frame(
  Modelo        = c("SVM Lineal", "SVM Radial", "SVM Polinomial"),
  Acc_Train_CV  = round(c(acc_train_lineal, acc_train_radial, acc_train_poly), 4),
  Acc_Test      = round(c(acc_lineal, acc_radial, acc_poly), 4),
  Diferencia    = round(c(acc_train_lineal - acc_lineal,
                          acc_train_radial - acc_radial,
                          acc_train_poly   - acc_poly), 4),
  Diagnostico   = ifelse(
    abs(c(acc_train_lineal - acc_lineal,
          acc_train_radial - acc_radial,
          acc_train_poly   - acc_poly)) > 0.05,
    "Sobreajuste probable", "Ajuste razonable"
  )
)

# Si tienes los valores de modelos anteriores, agregalos aqui:
# sobreajuste <- rbind(sobreajuste, data.frame(
#   Modelo = "Random Forest", Acc_Train_CV = X, Acc_Test = Y, Diferencia = X-Y, Diagnostico = ...
# ))

print(sobreajuste)

cat("\nNota: Para clasificacion, el sobreajuste se detecta comparando accuracy (y Kappa)\n",
    "en entrenamiento vs prueba. Una diferencia > 5 pp sugiere sobreajuste.\n",
    "Tambien se pueden comparar precision, recall y F1 por clase.\n")
```
